raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased XXXX XXXX XXXX  totaling {$18000.00} from the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ). Both bags were completely unused, in original condition, and within XXXX return window when I attempted to return them in person. 

My family and I physically visited the store within the 14-day return period and spoke with sales associate XXXX XXXX and the store manager. They initially offered an exchange, which confirms the bags were in acceptable condition, but when we requested a refund instead, they refused without justification. 

I also called XXXX customer service twice and was told they could not assist with the return. Later, I recorded a follow-up visit to the store, where the store manager admitted on video that we had come in during the return window to request a return. The items are still unused and in perfect condition. I submitted photos and video evidence to Macys XXXX XXXX ( Citibank ) during the dispute process. 

Despite this, my dispute was denied. Citibank claimed XXXX reported that I never communicated any intent to return the items. This is false, and I have documented proof to refute it. XXXX also refused to provide surveillance footage or written documentation regarding the denial. I am seeking a reversal of the {$18000.00} charge and believe both XXXX and Citibank acted in bad faith by misrepresenting the facts and denying a fair dispute review.
